user_prompt: add a `--flash` option, I don't trust clicking the IDE's flash option because it risks a rebuild that isn't secure
agent_reply: - Builds through `pio run -e thefly_sec1`, so the secure signing hook runs.
- Calls `esptool.py write_flash` directly, avoiding `pio run -t upload` and avoiding an IDE-triggered rebuild path.
I also fixed repeat-build idempotency: if the bootloader is already signed, the script re-signs from `bootloader-unsigned.bin` instead of accidentally appending another signature sector.
I did not actually flash hardware.
- Builds through `pio run -e thefly_sec1`, so the secure signing hook runs.
- Calls `esptool.py write_flash` directly, avoiding `pio run -t upload` and avoiding an IDE-triggered rebuild path.
I also fixed repeat-build idempotency: if the bootloader is already signed, the script re-signs from `bootloader-unsigned.bin` instead of accidentally appending another signature sector.
I did not actually flash hardware.

user_prompt: can you make the serial port selection work the same way as `tools\just_upload_it.py`
agent_reply: Done. `build_security.py` now uses the same serial-port selection style as [tools/just_upload_it.py](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/tools/just_upload_it.py:1):
- Prefers USB serial markers like `cp210`, `ch340`, `ftdi`, `silicon labs`, `esp32`, `espressif`
- Errors if no USB-backed COM port is found
- Prints all matches if multiple are found and uses the first one
Done. `build_security.py` now uses the same serial-port selection style as [tools/just_upload_it.py](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/tools/just_upload_it.py:1):
- Prefers USB serial markers like `cp210`, `ch340`, `ftdi`, `silicon labs`, `esp32`, `espressif`
- Errors if no USB-backed COM port is found
- Prints all matches if multiple are found and uses the first one
